# Accepted dependency advisories — audit allowlist
#
# Format: one GHSA advisory ID per line. Blank lines and lines starting with `#`
# are ignored. Each entry MUST carry a comment explaining WHY it is accepted and,
# where known, the condition that would let us drop it (a fixed version).
#
# This file is the ONLY escape hatch for the CI audit gate
# (.github/scripts/audit-gate.sh). Any HIGH or CRITICAL advisory NOT listed here
# fails the build. Keep this list as short as honesty allows — every entry is a
# documented, deliberate acceptance, not a mute button.
#
# Review this list whenever dependencies are bumped; drop entries the moment the
# advisory's patched version is reachable.

# --- vitest / vite (dev-only test runner; not shipped in any published package) ---
# Both advisories below come from the pinned `vitest@^2.1.0` dev dependency in
# packages/{crew,dispatch,memory}. They are exploitable only against the Vitest
# UI dev server / Vite dev server — neither of which we run in CI or ship to
# users (vitest + vite are devDependencies; published `dist/` carries no vite).
# The real fix is bumping vitest to >=3.2.6, a major upgrade across all three
# suites that is out of scope for this hardening pass and tracked separately.
# Drop both entries once vitest is on >=3.2.6 (which pulls vite >=6.4.3).
GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp  # critical · vitest <3.2.6 · Vitest UI server arbitrary file read/exec
GHSA-fx2h-pf6j-xcff  # high · vite <=6.4.2 · server.fs.deny bypass on Windows alternate paths
